content.claim: Dormitory (kollégium) capacity nationally exceeds demand, with far more places than applicants and declining demand, suggesting dormitory availability is not a binding constraint for 6-/8-year track expansion.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Figure is aggregate across all secondary school types and dormitory users, not disaggregated for 6-/8-year track applicants specifically
Age-appropriateness of existing kollégium infrastructure and welfare supervision models for 10-12-year-old boarders (versus typical 14+ boarders) is not directly quantified
Declining demand could reflect demographic factors rather than surplus capacity
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
